Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1: Search Linear issues by project (proj_expense_autocategorizer) to identify all open and in-progress engineering tickets related to the ExpenseBot auto-categorizer remediation, including their current statuses, assignees, and due dates.
OE2: Read Linear comments on key remediation tickets (ENG-211, ENG-212, ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-216, ENG-217, ENG-220) to determine the current status of work involving Lena Bjorkstrom and Dmitri Volkov, including any recorded blockers, dependencies, or progress updates.
OE3: Search Slack messages in the #engineering channel (C003) and read Linear comments on ENG-217 (pipeline hardening ticket, where Dmitri posted the audit results) for the ExpenseBot pilot audit findings, including the completed audit of all 47 auto-processed expenses, the breakdown of 5 false rejections (Vectral) and 3 false approvals (Mosaic), and the net financial exposure of $885.
OE4: Search Slack messages, Linear comments (ENG-216), or email threads (Dmitri's findings email to Marcus) to discover the details of the three flagged duplicate receipt submissions — Kevin Tran $89 keyboard (Vectral, paid), Andre Washington $89 keyboard (Mosaic, pending approval), and Jordan Ekwueme $45 monitor stand (BrightLoop, pending approval) — including vendor (TechComfort Supply), duplicate hash flags, payment statuses, and total exposure ($223). The underlying Airtable stipend records (tblStipends00001) contain the source-of-truth data.
OE5: Search emails for correspondence related to the ExpenseBot audit (e.g., Samira's audit summary to Elena, Dmitri's duplicate receipt findings email to Marcus), the AWS cost spike escalation (Marcus to Samira, Samira's root cause analysis), and the DOT compliance inquiry (Linda Castellano's formal letter, Chloe's hazmat audit reply, Swift Relocations placarding certificate exchange).
OE6: Search Slack messages in the #root-cause-aws-spike channel (C009) and #finance channel (C005) for the AWS cost spike investigation from March 2026, including the root cause (Vantage Distributed enterprise onboarding of 1,200+ users starting March 21), the total bill ($42,870 vs ~$14,300 baseline), and the current status of remediation actions (cost alerts, budget tagging, pricing model review).
OE7: Search Linear issues on team_operations for tickets related to DOT compliance or hazmat documentation (project_dot_compliance_audit) to determine whether any outstanding documentation work involves or is blocked by the engineering team.
OE8: Search Slack messages across relevant channels and email correspondence for updates on the DOT hazmat placarding certificate gap for the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter shipment, including whether Chloe Vance has obtained the signed certificate from Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations.
OE9: Send a consolidated engineering status email from samira.tariq@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com with marcus.thorne@moveops.com copied, summarizing the ExpenseBot remediation status across all workstreams: open ticket statuses, audit results ($885 financial exposure), duplicate receipt findings ($223), AWS cost spike root cause and remediation, and DOT compliance documentation status.
OE10: Post an engineering status update in the #engineering Slack channel (C003) summarizing the current state of all remediation workstreams, including ticket progress, audit results, and outstanding action items.
OE11: Add status comments or updates to each open Linear ticket related to the ExpenseBot auto-categorizer remediation project (ENG-211, ENG-212, ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-216, ENG-217, ENG-218, ENG-219, ENG-220) reflecting where things currently stand.
OE12: Send a status update email from samira.tariq@moveops.com to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com explaining the remediation timeline for the Vectral Systems pilot account, including the current state of the config fix (ENG-214), pipeline hardening (ENG-217), and the impact on the expense review process.
OE13: Send a status update email from samira.tariq@moveops.com to emeka.diallo@moveops.com explaining the remediation timeline for the Mosaic Robotics pilot account, including the current state of the config fix (ENG-215), the duplicate receipt situation affecting Andre Washington, and the impact on the expense review process.
OE14: Create a CRM engagement note on the Vectral Systems account (company_vectral) documenting the current ExpenseBot remediation status, pilot timeline, and impact on Vectral's expense review workflow.
OE15: Create a CRM engagement note on the Mosaic Robotics account (company_mosaic) documenting the current ExpenseBot remediation status, pilot timeline, duplicate receipt findings, and impact on Mosaic's expense review workflow.

8 The email sent to Elena and Marcus reports the revised financial exposure from the ExpenseBot audit as approximately $885 (or similar) rather than the earlier estimate of $1,550.
outcome
 ✗✓✓✗✗✗ 33%
✗Run #1The email to Elena and Marcus reports '$1,550 confirmed (Mosaic false approvals)' as the financial exposure and '$1,773 total from engineering issues.' It never references the revised ~$885 figure. The agent failed to identify that the audit revised the exposure downward (distinguishing overages from total amounts and finding the third case, Kenji Nakamura), which should yield approximately $400 + $350 + $135 = $885.
✓Run #2The email sent to Elena and Marcus explicitly states 'Net financial exposure: $885 (revised down from initial $1,550 estimate after full review)', meeting this criterion.
✓Run #3The email sent to Elena and Marcus explicitly states 'Financial exposure: $885 total for Mosaic (not the initial $1,550 estimate — the third incorrect approval was a $135 coworking day-pass for Kenji Nakamura),' correctly reporting the revised ~$885 figure.
✗Run #4The email to Elena and Marcus reports the financial exposure as '$1,550+' for false approvals (pre-audit estimate), totaling '~$1,773' combined. The criterion requires a revised post-audit figure of approximately $885 ($400 standing desk overage + $350 coworking + $135 coworking day passes), which is different from the full approved amounts. The agent used the pre-audit $1,550 estimate rather than the audit-revised ~$885 figure.
✗Run #5The email to Elena and Marcus reports financial exposure as '$1,550 in false approvals + $350/mo ongoing coworking risk', not the revised ~$885 figure. The agent did not perform the audit calculation that would arrive at $885 ($400 overage + $350 coworking + $135 day passes), instead reporting the full approved amounts as the exposure.
✗Run #6The email to Elena and Marcus reports 'Combined direct financial exposure from auto-categorizer bugs: ~$1,550' — the original estimate, not the revised ~$885 figure (which reflects $400 standing desk overage + $350 coworking + $135 day passes from the detailed audit breakdown). The agent did not report the corrected post-audit figure.
9 The email sent to Elena and Marcus, or the #engineering Slack post, identifies duplicate receipt submissions totaling approximately $223 (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1Both the email to Elena/Marcus and the #engineering Slack post explicitly reference duplicate receipt submissions totaling $223 ($89 Kevin Tran, $89 Andre Washington, $45 Jordan Ekwueme).
✓Run #2The email to Elena/Marcus and the Slack post both reference the duplicate receipt finding totaling $223 ($89 disbursed, $134 on hold).
✓Run #3The email to Elena and Marcus explicitly states 'Duplicate receipts (cross-client): $223 ($89 disbursed, $134 on hold)' and the Slack post also references 'Total exposure: $223 ($89 paid, $134 on hold).'
✓Run #4Both the consolidated email to Elena/Marcus and the engineering Slack post explicitly mention 'Total exposure: $223 ($89 disbursed, $134 held by finance)' for the duplicate receipts from TechComfort Supply across three client accounts.
✓Run #5The email to Elena and Marcus states 'duplicate receipts: $223 total' and the Slack post mentions '$223 in duplicate receipt risk'. The $223 figure from TechComfort Supply submissions is correctly identified in both the email and Slack post.
✓Run #6The email to Elena explicitly states 'Financial exposure: $223 total ($89 already disbursed, $134 on hold by finance)' for the duplicate receipts. The Slack post similarly references '$223'.
10 The email sent to Elena and Marcus, or the #engineering Slack post, identifies the AWS cost spike root cause as Vantage Distributed enterprise onboarding with the March bill approximately $42,870 (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1Both the email and the Slack post explicitly identify Vantage Distributed enterprise onboarding as the AWS cost spike root cause, with the March bill at ~$42,870 vs ~$14,300 baseline.
